content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
September 4, 1997
Gary M. Baum, City Attorney
City of Morgan Hill
17555 Peak Avenue
Morgan Hill, California 95037
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-97-421
Dear Mr. Baum:
This letter responds to your request for informal advice on behalf of Councilmember
Hedy Chang about the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
Your advice request poses a number of hypothetical questions about situations
Councilmember Chang, a real estate agent, expects to arise and which may raise conflict of
interest issues. The Act’s conflict-of-interest provisions are designed to be applied in particular,
concrete factual situations. Because your request is hypothetical, we decline to answer the
questions posed in your advice request. (Regulation 18329(c)(4), copy enclosed.) Please find
enclosed a number ofprevious advice letters which should be helpful to Councilmember Chang
as a councilmember who is employed as a real estate agent. Also, please find enclosed a copy of
the Commission’s conflict of interest guide.
If a question about a potential conflict of interest arises regarding councilmember Chang,
in the context of a particular decision, we encourage you to seek advice at that time. If you have
any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JV:jlw
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
I:\LEGALDIV\Advice Ltrs\97421 .wpd
By:
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660
